headline: Exclusive-OpenAI builds first chip with Broadcom and TSMC, scales back foundry ambition
summary: OpenAI is working with Broadcom and TSMC to build its first in-house chip designed to support its artificial intelligence systems, while adding AMD chips alongside Nvidia chips to meet its surging infrastructure demands, sources told Reuters.  OpenAI, the fast-growing company behind ChatGPT, has examined a range of options to diversify chip supply and reduce costs.  OpenAI considered building everything in-house and raising capital for an expensive plan to build a network of factories known as foundries for chip manufacturing.

headline: Harris defends CHIPS Act ahead of election, while Trump criticizes it
summary: The CHIPS and Science Act was passed with bipartisan support in August 2022 as a means to jumpstart and bolster domestic semiconductor manufacturing within the US. Ahead of the 2024 Election Day on Tuesday, Vice President Kamala Harris defended the investments made into American chipmaking and the broader domestic industrial sector. Yahoo Finance special reporter Akiko Fujita highlights the political tensions arising around future of the CHIPS Act following comments made by House Speaker Mike Johnson (R) and former President and Republican candidate Donald Trump, as well as the potential impacts the US election results could have on major semiconductor players. Catch Yahoo Finances interview with Intel (INTC) CEO Pat Gelsinger on the chip companys third quarter earnings results and its CHIPS Act fundings. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Catalysts here. This post was written by Luke Carberry Mogan.

headline: Repositioning for 2025? Check out chip and small-cap stocks.
summary: US stocks (^DJI,^GSPC, ^IXIC) are trading near record highs ahead of the new year. How should investors position their portfolios in 2025? Bokeh Capital Partners founder and chief investment officer Kimberly Forrest joins Seana Smith and Brad Smith on Morning Brief to share her 2025 portfolio repositioning strategy, focusing on semiconductor stocks and small-cap allocation. I would look at that super semiconductor space and make some adjustments, Forrest tells Yahoo Finance, explaining, It feels like semiconductors that are targeted on AI seem very overweight, and you should take a look at that. Names like Nvidia (NVDA), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturer Company (TSM), and Broadcom (AVGO) are among some of the top year-to-date gainers in the chip space. The strategist highlights potential opportunities in small-caps with a market capitalization of at least $1 billion. Those mid-size companies are kind of forgotten recently and theyre also misunderstood. She adds, A lot of the lore on Wall Street says that mid-cap companies and small-cap companies mostly serve where they are founded in the US, and I disagree with that. I think a lot of the mid-caps are global companies... and I think those are the ones that you should be on the lookout for. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief here. This post was written by Naomi Buchanan.
